logic_gaps.description: 5 dir-08 playbook scenarios (058/059/061/062/063) contained stale 'exists under .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/deep-loop/' references for review-depth-validator/convergence/graph.vitest.ts despite AF-0023 migrating those test paths. AF-0023 had patched the `## Commands` rg-grep lines but missed the prose-style 'exists under' references in SOURCE METADATA blocks (line 30 of each file). **Resolved inline during iter 6**: 5 files patched to use deep-loop-runtime/tests/integration/ via Python substitution. File 060 reducer ref intentionally retained (the test DID stay in system-spec-kit).

review-depth-reducer.vitest.ts remains in `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/deep-loop/` while sibling tests (review-depth-validator/convergence/graph) moved to `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/tests/integration/` during 118 FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP. Architectural inconsistency: 3 of 4 review-depth tests migrated to deep-loop-runtime, 1 (reducer) did not. This may be intentional (reducer logic lives in deep-review's own scripts/reduce-state.cjs, so its test belongs near the spec-kit MCP harness) or oversight. Doc reference in 060 IS accurate; finding is about migration completeness not doc drift.

changelog/v1.3.1.0.md references `deep-review-expected behavior-parity.vitest.ts` (likely a typo or stale name); actual file is `deep-review-contract-parity.vitest.ts`. Historical changelog preserved per AF-0019 chronological-fidelity policy; finding recorded for awareness but no doc edit.

novel_findings: Phase 5 self-resolved one of its own findings: LG-0025 P0 was fixed inline during iter 6 surfacing (5 files patched). Demonstrates the loop's ability to close gaps as they're found when they fall in the doc-edit scope (ADR-002 surgical-edit permits P0/P1 immediate fixes).

LG-0025 reveals an AF-0023 migration gap: I patched grep-command paths in Phase 2 but missed prose-style 'exists under' source-metadata references. Future migration sweeps should scan BOTH command lines AND source-metadata blocks.

All 3 iter 6 findings are NOVEL (zero duplicates of iters 1-5). Cross-skill dependency dimension surfaced a different class of bugs (path-ref staleness in source-metadata) than the doc-vs-code drift class of iters 1-5.
notes: Iter 6 RQ: cross-skill dependency drift. Wall-clock 140s. 3 novel gaps surfaced (zero duplicates — different class of bug than iters 1-5). LG-0025 P0 fixed inline during iter 6 (5 dir-08 files patched). LG-0026 P1 is migration-inconsistency note; doc is accurate. LG-0027 P2 historical changelog typo preserved.
